data.title: Coca-Cola Ditches Costa Coffee Sale — What It Signals for KO Investors
data.description:
Coca-Cola (KO) has reportedly scrapped plans to sell its Costa Coffee business after bids from private equity firms fell short of expectations. The decision suggests that the beverage giant sees greater long-term value in retaining and expanding the brand rather than selling at current valuations. For KO investors, the move signals that by walking away from low offers, Coca-Cola is demonstrating disciplined capital allocation, a stance generally positive for shareholder value. That said, Costa remains a relatively small part of Coca-Cola's overall business, making the near-term impact on KO stock limited.

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Slip After S&P 500 (SPX) Retreats From Record Levels
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged lower ahead of Wednesday's session after the S&P 500 (SPX) pulled back from record highs reached earlier in the week. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down a modest 0.11%, 0.27%, and 0.17%, respectively, at 12:06 a.m. EDT on January 14.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading session, all three major indexes closed in the red. The S&P 500 fell 0.2%, the Dow dropped nearly 400 points, and the Nasdaq Composite edged down 0.1%.

Financial stocks led the losses. Shares of JPMorgan Chase (JPM) fell more than 4% after its fourth-quarter investment banking fees appeared weaker than expected. Goldman Sachs (GS) and Bank of America (BAC) also declined.

On the inflation front, December's Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 2.7% from a year earlier. Investors will get another update on inflation with the release of the December Producer Price Index (PPI) before the market opens.

Earnings will also be in focus. Bank of America, Wells Fargo (WFC), and Citigroup (C) are all scheduled to report their quarterly results before the opening bell.

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Salesforce (CRM) Turns Slackbot into a Powerful AI Assistant in Major Platform Upgrade
data.description:
Salesforce (CRM) has launched its redesigned Slackbot, turning the chat helper into a full‑fledged AI work assistant built directly inside Slack. The rollout marks a major step in the software giant's plan to make Slack the main hub for its enterprise AI tools, supported by its broader Agentforce 360 strategy.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company said Slackbot has already been deployed internally and is the fastest-adopted product in the company's history, with some teams reporting time savings of up to 20 hours per week.

Companies like IBM (IBM), General Motors (GM), Amazon (AMZN), OpenAI, and Anthropic were among early testers during the preview phase.

Salesforce Turns Slackbot into an AI Work Assistant

Salesforce is giving Slack a major upgrade, with a new, smarter Slackbot that does much more than reminders. The new version acts as a personal AI agent, able to understand work context, find information from chats and files, and even handle actions like scheduling meetings or drafting documents, all without leaving Slack.

Among other key capabilities, the Slackbot can summarize threads, retrieve documents, and generate meeting notes. Also, it can pull customer data from Salesforce systems to prepare users for calls or account reviews.

Moreover, Slack executives say the bot performs real‑time retrieval rather than training on customer data, addressing concerns about privacy and data handling.

The update is now generally available for Business+ and Enterprise+ customers, with a phased rollout continuing through January and February.

Overall, the revamped Slackbot reflects Salesforce's goal of making Slack the main entry point for an AI-powered workplace where people and AI work together smoothly.

Is CRM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, CRM stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 31 Buys, 10 Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $326.95, the average Salesforce stock price target implies a 35.63% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Cathie Wood Keeps Loading Up on This Biotech Stock — What's Driving the Steady Buying?
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) continued to buy shares of Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) on Tuesday, extending a recent buying trend in the stock. The firm's continued buying suggests it remains confident in the company's longer-term prospects, even as biotech stocks remain volatile.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In its latest trade update for January 13, 2026, ARK bought 40,067 shares of Intellia. Most of the shares were added through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK), with a smaller portion purchased via the ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG). The total value of the buy was about $458,000.

(Note: To see more of Cathie Wood's January 9 trades, click here — NTLA, PSNL, KMTUY, ILMN: Cathie Wood Buys Early Biotech Stocks, Cuts Stakes in Illumina.)

This was not a one-time move. A day earlier, ARK added 99,292 shares of Intellia, and earlier last week, it bought 216,411 shares. While Tuesday's trade was smaller, the steady buying shows ARK is still adding to the position.

The recent buying comes ahead of a potential catalyst. Intellia is set to present at the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference on January 14, a forum where biotech companies often provide updates on their pipelines and plans. Investors will be watching closely for any updates on Intellia's CRISPR programs and next steps.

Is Intellia a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Intellia stock based on nine Buys, 12 Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months. At $16.50, the average NTLA stock price target implies a 40.55% upside potential. Year-to-date, shares of the company have gained 30.6%.

data.title: Strategy Stock (MSTR) Gains 6% after Rare Insider Buy
data.description:
Strategy (MSTR) stock jumped more than 6% on Tuesday, with the move largely driven by a rare insider purchase, along with short-term relief around index eligibility and renewed attention on the company's Bitcoin holdings. The rebound comes after a steep selloff, with the stock down about 42% over the past three months.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Insider Steps In After Years of Selling

One of the biggest signals came from inside the company. Director Carl Rickertsen bought 5,000 shares of Strategy, according to a January 12 filing with the SEC. The purchase totaled about $779,000, with shares acquired at roughly $156 each.

In recent years, Rickertsen had only sold shares, making this his first open-market purchase since 2022.

MSCI Relief Offers Short-Term Support

The insider buying also comes just days after Strategy avoided a potential setback linked to MSCI. MSCI has been reviewing whether companies with more than 50% of their balance sheet tied to cryptocurrencies should remain eligible for its indices.

Given Strategy's heavy exposure to Bitcoin, the company was at risk. While MSCI has delayed any removal for now, the issue remains under review, offering short-term relief rather than a final resolution.

Strategy Adds to Its Bitcoin Holdings

Separately, Strategy added more Bitcoin to its balance sheet. The company said it bought 13,627 Bitcoin in one week, its largest purchase since July 2025. This brings Strategy's total Bitcoin holdings to nearly 687,410 coins, keeping it the world's largest corporate holder.

As a result, the stock continues to move closely with Bitcoin's price, which remains a key factor investors watch.

Is MSTR Stock a Buy Now?

On TipRanks, Strategy stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 12 Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Strategy price target of $453.17 implies an impressive 162% upside potential from current levels. Meanwhile, MSTR shares have lost 49% over the past year.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Warner Bros. Bidding War – Netflix Eyes All-Cash Offer, Paramount Skydance Sues Them
data.description:
The media industry is witnessing one of the biggest high stakes bidding wars for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). Netflix (NFLX) is contemplating an all-cash offer to acquire WBD's studio and streaming assets, while Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has escalated the dispute with a lawsuit aimed at blocking the deal or altering terms.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

These developments could reshape content pipelines and streaming competition, and face regulatory scrutiny. Since the bidding process became public three months ago, WBD shares have surged 60.5%, while NFLX stock has fallen 25.7% and PSKY shares have dropped 29.8%.

Netflix Weighs All-Cash Bid

WBD's board currently favors Netflix's cash-and-stock offer, valued at $82.7 billion. However, Paramount's allegations of misconduct have prompted Netflix to explore an all-cash alternative to simplify negotiations and reduce financing risks.

Under the proposed terms, WBD shareholders would receive $23.25 in cash plus $4.50 in Netflix stock per share, based on Netflix's 15-day average price. If Netflix shares fall below $97.91, shareholders would get 0.0460 Netflix shares per WBD share. Netflix closed at $90.32 on Tuesday, after hitting a low of $89.07.

Analysts note that an all-cash deal could bolster Netflix's content strategy, grant control over WBD's film and TV studios plus streaming assets, and accelerate international expansion and divestitures of Warner's TV networks.

Paramount Skydance Will Not Give Up

In response to the evolving bids, Paramount Skydance has filed a lawsuit in Delaware's Court of Chancery, alleging improper conduct by WBD's board. Paramount CEO David Ellison seeks more details on why the board rejected its "superior" all-cash offer of $30 per share.

Ellison has threatened a proxy fight to replace WBD's board at the 2026 annual meeting, potentially forcing negotiations and bylaw changes. These could mandate a shareholder vote to spin off Discovery Global in Q3 2026. Legal experts say court rulings and regulatory reviews could delay or alter the deal. Only shareholders owning 20% of WBD stock for at least a year can call a special meeting.

Paramount Skydance has made eight bids, including one with a firm promise from David Ellison's dad, Larry Ellison, Oracle (ORCL) co-founder and the world's fourth-richest person. Yet, WBD board keeps rejecting the offers, calling them too low, risky, and worse than Netflix's. They also said there is too much doubt about whether Paramount can actually finish the deal.

NFLX or PSKY: Which Is the Better Media Stock?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to determine which media company is currently preferred by analysts.

Analysts remain cautious on both stocks amid Warner Bros. Discovery buyout chaos. However, NFLX stock carries a "Moderate Buy" consensus versus PSKY's "Moderate Sell," implying analysts favor Netflix, with greater upside potential over the next 12 months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: TSM Stock Near Record Highs—Is It Still a Buy Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., or TSMC, (TSM) is set to report its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings on Thursday, January 15. While the company's Q4 revenue has already topped estimates, investors will be closely focused on margins and profitability. With the stock trading near record highs, near-term upside may be more limited. Still, TSMC's bullish outlook is supported by strong demand for advanced chips used in AI data centers, high-performance computing, and next-generation smartphones. Over the past 12 months, TSM shares have risen roughly 65%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, TSMC is the world's largest contract chipmaker. The company supplies advanced semiconductors to top tech giants, including Apple (AAPL) and Nvidia (NVDA).

What to Expect from TSM's Q4 Earnings

Wall Street expects TSMC to post earnings of $2.9 per share, nearly 30% higher than a year ago. Meanwhile, revenue is forecast at $32.97 billion, up from $26.88 billion in the same quarter last year.

TSMC has already shared its monthly sales last week, with fourth-quarter revenue jumping about 20% year over year to roughly NT$1.05 trillion ($33 billion), beating analyst expectations and landing near the top of its guidance range of $32.2 billion to $33.4 billion. In the full earnings report, investors will be closely focused on profitability.

Is TSM Stock Worth Buying for the Long Term?

Because leading AI chipmakers such as Nvidia, AMD (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO) outsource manufacturing to TSMC, the company benefits from the AI boom regardless of which chips dominate. This makes TSMC a more stable, lower-risk way to gain AI exposure. Its technology leadership, pricing power, and deep relationships with major customers support record revenue and profit potential, with gross margins near 60% (57.8% in the third quarter), reinforcing earnings strength.

In addition, its shares trade at a cheaper valuation than many peers. TSM's FWD P/E ratio is at 31.02, very close to the sector's average of 32.22.

Overall, analysts remain highly bullish, expecting another record fourth-quarter profit driven by massive demand for AI infrastructure—supporting the case for a potential stock buy.

TSM Stock Forecast

According to TipRanks, TSM stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on six Buys and one Hold assigned in the last three months. At $313.45, TSMC's average stock price target implies a downside of 5.36% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Why This Top Analyst Says Buy These 5 Chip Stocks for 2026 — and Urges Caution on Intel and AMD
data.description:
Bernstein believes the AI chip rally still has more room to run. In a new note, top semiconductor analyst Stacy Rasgon said there is "little reason to change our broad perspective," as AI spending remains strong and demand for chipmaking equipment continues to improve. In short, Bernstein sees ongoing investment in AI as a durable trend rather than a short-term spike. It is worth noting that Rasgon ranks 120 out of more than 10,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks and has a success rate of 68%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Against that backdrop, the firm highlighted five U.S. chip stocks it sees as best positioned to benefit through 2026.

  • Nvidia (NVDA): Bernstein's top pick is Nvidia. Rasgon said AI spending "shows no signs of slowing" and added that Nvidia "appears to be pulling farther ahead" of competitors. The firm prefers Nvidia's product roadmap, arguing the company continues to widen its lead in high-end AI workloads.
  • Broadcom (AVGO): Broadcom is Bernstein's second top pick. The firm sees it as a key beneficiary of AI-related demand across networking and custom chips. It also noted that valuations remain attractive as earnings estimates continue to move higher.
  • Applied Materials (AMAT): Bernstein remains bullish on semiconductor equipment makers and rates Applied Materials Outperform. The firm said it would "own semicap even as valuations reach nosebleed levels," citing rising odds of positive revisions to wafer-fab equipment forecasts as AI-driven capacity expands.
  • Lam Research (LRCX): Lam Research also carries an Outperform rating. Bernstein noted that semicap stocks "powered to new highs" in 2025 but believes demand tied to advanced chip manufacturing can continue to support earnings growth.
  • Qualcomm (QCOM): Rounding out the list is Qualcomm. Bernstein said it continues to favor Qualcomm, despite Apple's (AAPL) plans to rely less on Qualcomm modems, as the firm sees improving fundamentals once Apple-related concerns ease..

What Bernstein Is Avoiding

Bernstein is more cautious on other areas of the chip sector. The firm said analog chipmakers look expensive, while a clear recovery is still hard to see. It also said Intel (INTC) fundamentals are "unattractive," even with recent political support. Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) remains rated Market Perform, as Bernstein continues to prefer Nvidia's roadmap and execution.

Which Chip Stock Is the Best Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the seven stocks mentioned above, Nvidia stands out, offering the highest upside at about 43% and carrying a Strong Buy rating. AMD follows with roughly 29% upside and a Strong Buy consensus. Broadcom also holds a Strong Buy rating, with around 30% upside, and ranks highest on the Smart Score, pointing to strong backing from analysts, hedge funds, and broader market signals.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Government Clears Nvidia (NVDA) to Export H200 Microchips to China
data.description:
The government of U.S. President Donald Trump has eased restrictions placed on the export of Nvidia's (NVDA) H200 microchips to China.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Multiple media reports say that the Trump administration is implementing a rule that will enable shipments of Nvidia's advanced AI chips to China. However, some conditions remain on the export of Nvidia processors to China.

Before any H200 chips can be sent to China, the U.S. Commerce Department will require that the shipments undergo review by a third-party testing laboratory to confirm their artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities. Also, Nvidia must certify that there are sufficient H200 chips available in the U.S. before any can be sent to China.

The Impact of China on Nvidia

The regulatory change and clearance for Nvidia to ship its H200 microchips to China is the latest twist in the ongoing drama involving trade between Washington, D.C. and Beijing. U.S. policy regarding the export of advanced semiconductor technology to China has shifted several times in recent years.

At various times, Nvidia has been banned from shipping microchips to China that can be used for AI models and applications, with the U.S. government citing national security concerns. However, the Trump administration has sought to allow Nvidia to sell to China the H200 chip, which is based on older and less powerful technology.

At the same time, China has threatened to restrict the purchase of Nvidia's processors within the Asian nation. For its part, Nvidia has repeatedly said that China remains an important and potentially lucrative market for the company.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

The stock of Nvidia has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 41 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 39 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $264.97 implies 42.60% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Notches Up Despite Plummeting Cybertruck Sales Figures
data.description:
Electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) has an ambitious schedule afoot for 2026. With some referring to it as Tesla's "Prove-It Year," it has plans to roll out everything from robotics to Roadsters. And as more information emerges about 2025, we discover just how vital those plans really are. Tesla's Cybertruck is not selling well. This left investors cautiously optimistic, as Tesla shares gained fractionally in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The numbers that emerged are troubling. Cox Automotive data says that Tesla sold just 20,237 Cybertrucks in the United States in all of 2025. That is about half of what Tesla sold back in 2024, and the numbers only got worse the farther they went. The fourth quarter of 2025 saw sales drop 68%. And given that Elon Musk was out to sell over 10 times that number at 250,000 a year, it is clear the Cybertruck is not the sales powerhouse some had hoped for.

While there were plenty of reservations for the Cybertruck, they did not pan out into sales. It did not help that the original price for a Cybertruck was supposed to be $40,000, but ballooned to nearly double that before launch. Throw in 10 different recalls in two years—including one nightmarish possibility of an accelerator pedal that could get stuck at full—and the strange-looking, occasionally dangerous almost-pickup just did not work.

Who Can Dethrone Tesla?

Thus, some are looking to who could ultimately take Tesla's place in the market. Some companies are clear possibilities. Other electric vehicle makers might qualify, like XPeng (XPEV) or even Ford (F), which recently rolled out plans for electric vehicles with the least expensive motors around.

But one report suggests the real Tesla dethroner is none other than Nvidia (NVDA). The launch of Alpamayo represents a significant threat to Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) concept. It has the potential, reports note, to become the Android to Tesla's iOS. If businesses are willing to license Alpamayo over FSD, then Nvidia could indeed become a Tesla-killer, on at least one front.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 13.27% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $401.93 per share implies 10.16% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson
